no live target scoring or grader contact authorized
no key-content exposure authorized; the C-beta key remains closed in owner-private custody
spend ceiling USD 8.00; absolute attempt ceiling 344; output cap 768 tokens per attempt; <=32 transport retries
any change to a pre-R14 controlling frozen artifact voids this authorization; repairs are confined to the R14-arc construction files
bounded_unknowns: Serving-provider identity on OpenRouter is dynamic (the haiku probe was served by Bedrock); the census records actuals, and family identity is declared at the model level, not the serving level — this residue is named, not resolved.

kimi-k3's reasoning behavior under the 768-token cap is unknown at scale; reasoning exhaustion lands as determinate null-content census entries, and a stratum degraded by them is a reportable outcome, not a rerun license.
This record cannot prove the rerun will complete inside the window or ceilings; an honest partial stop with a written census remains a determinate outcome.

rationale: Owner route-substitution ruling (AskUserQuestion interview, Claude Code Fable 5 chair session, 2026-07-18, America/Sao_Paulo), after emission attempt-01 honest-stopped at $0.00 on three independently verified direct-route failures (API-id mismatch, insufficient_quota, authentication_error) and owner-authorized probes verified all three subjects live via OpenRouter with haiku/luna prices identical to the frozen r6 basis and kimi-k3 repriced from free to 3.00/15.00 USD/MTok, worst-case still far under the unchanged 8.00 ceiling. The owner's standing intent — the lab's funded, sandbox-verified OpenRouter route — replaces the three direct routes in a single sealed amendment, with a fresh 12-hour window and every custody, firewall, and receipts-before-contact requirement carried forward unchanged.
